meaning: n.衰老老人adj.老年医学的,老人的
examples: As any geriatric ward shows, that is not the same as to confer enduring mobility, awareness and autonomy.
I'm not a geriatric yet, you know!
There is a question mark over the future of geriatric care.
On a recent day, Marcy Smith, the geriatric social worker, helps a thin, distinguished-looking 91-year-old man.
The pilot study was published in January in the International Journal of Geriatric Psychiatry.
